entry.title: Dangerous Goods Check for Transportation with Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA comprises of :

  • Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance
  • Safety Data Sheet and Label Management
  • Dangerous Goods Management

(Help documentation SAP S/4HANA -> Enterprise Business Applications -> R&D / Engineering -> Product Compliance)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/35751be3d6ee423197492574e016a512/679a1d3744d34b43832da6890b9095c0.html?locale=en-US

 From the point of view of Transportation, Dangerous Goods Management is the focus as it deals with classifying products according to dangerous goods regulations and the dangerous goods data which is integrated into sales, delivery and transportation processes, where the information is used for transport compliance checks.

Dangerous Goods Check in Transportation Management can also be done based on Product Safety & Stewardship (PS&S) functionality. 

Few vital points to remember when using Product Compliance based Dangerous Goods Checks:

  • What is transported is a "Packaged Product" (in Product compliance there is a separation of Unpackaged and Packaged product)
  • Transport Regulations (example ADR, RID, ADN, IATA, IMDG, etc..) which are mode-of-transport specific and the countries party to those regulations decide if or not the Packed Product is transported

Some important Business Roles are:

Role Description

SAP_BR_PRODSTWRD_SPLST_PCLProduct Stewardship Specialist – Product Compliance
SAP_BR_MD_SPECIALIST_PCLMaster Data Specialist – Product Compliance
SAP_BR_DG_SPLST_PCLDangerous Goods Specialist – Product Compliance

Identifying Material as "relevant" for Compliance Check

This needs to be done in the "Manage Product Master Data" App.

Inbuilt workflow will initiate a request to manage "Basic compliance data of the Unpackaged/Packaged Product"

Here the important information about the Compliance Purposes is stored. Compliance Purpose includes the different Regulations which would be applied. Here one needs to be aware of the Regulations for different modes of transport and the respective geographies.

Below is an example of Product from Transportation point of view

 

 In next step the DG classification and Transport permissions needs to be maintained which is also called as the Classification of Product

As shown below, once you know the UN Classification of your product, all related information is readily available from the system and is auto-filled (Phrases

App "Compliance Information of Products" presents the maintained information, this app can be used by business users

 

 

Transportation Process view:

Sales Order provides information on the Dangerous Goods checks in the form of item and header "Status".

  Related Freight Unit provides the same in the form of "Blocks" and item "Status" 

 

 Delivery document created also makes the DG Checks based on countries and Mode-of-Transports involved in Transportation

 On Transportation Management side the "Actual" mode of Transport can be changed during the Execution Document Creation, at this point in time the Compliance Checks are performed again and necessary blocks are applied.

Happy Reading !!!

 

Note:  I have demonstrated the functionality in this blog post using system screen images with relevant data for ease of understanding for the readers. It should be noted that these screens and the data is from SAP’s internal test systems and has no relation to any external source

entry.title: Futuristic Aerospace or Defense BTP Data Mesh Layer using Collibra, Next Labs ABAC/DAM, IAG and GRC
entry.content:
Background

In this blog, we will explore few ideas for creating a domain-centric data mesh using SAP components, without engaging in the debate of whether data fabric or data mesh is the right approach.

We will focus on a futuristic use case within the Aerospace or Defense industry, which demands stringent data governance due to compliance requirements such as ITAR, EAR, BAFA, DOE 810, NERC/CIP, and SEC. Additionally, safeguarding intellectual property is a critical concern as business growth often relies on increased collaboration, both internally and externally, spanning product and engineering, supply chain, cross industry- partnerships, and joint ventures. I am happy to hear your ideas too!

Business Problem

Aerospace and defense customers often face challenges in keeping up with rapid technological advancements due to the burden of data debts and the need to comply with stringent legal and regulatory data security requirements.

This challenge is further amplified when dealing with legacy ERP systems, as identifying and restricting sensitive data becomes complex, hindering the end-to-end data lifecycle management. Moreover, the Aerospace and defense industry grapples with the formidable challenge of reducing IT costs, as the presence of significant data risks renders offshore operations an impractical option.

Let's consider the example of a product manager based in the United States, working for a US corporation. Their product is subject to ITAR regulations but has both government and commercial applications.

In order to comply with the business rule, the access to ITAR data in SAP should only be granted to US persons while they are in US locations. However, when this product manager is on a business trip to Singapore, meeting with suppliers at their APAC regional headquarters, exposing material data, CAD drawings, or BOMs stored in SAP would violate ITAR regulations.

In the context of a UK energy company establishing a joint venture with a local company in China to cater to the emerging market, an added layer of data security is required based on location. This ensures that access to BOM items and intellectual property not related to the joint venture is restricted, safeguarding sensitive information and preserving the integrity of the collaboration.

In the context of the aerospace and defense , "Data is not only the ammunition that fuels engines but it is also an the armor that protects international and national peace"

BTP SAP S/4 HANA Data Mesh Pattern

To solve the problem described above, we will hypothetically integrate Collibra, Next Labs ABAC/DAM, IAG and GRC  and SAP S/4 HANA  to provide data insights to users without compromising  data security requirements. Please note that is is hypothetical pattern and we have to review and apply it according to client specific data security requirements.

Solution ComponentsUsage
CollibraThis component can be used to document and define data governance policies, meta data catalogue, end to end data lineage, data quality KPI's, data protection and data privacy rules for bill of materials.
SAP IAG SAP IAG can handle the user access request and provisioning workflows to authenticate and authorize the user identity with the client's identity provider (ex: Active Directory)
SAP GRCSAP GRC can provide the necessary controls and policies for access management and perform risk analysis and segregation of duties (SoD) checks, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements across all SAP applications in the landscape.
Next Labs ABAC/DAM

Next Labs ABAC/DAM provides robust protection against unauthorized access to sensitive SAP data by implementing fine-grained access controls.

These controls can be applied at the level of individual data attributes or data ranges, enabling customers to safeguard their data while meeting compliance requirements. By examining the attributes of the data being accessed, the context of the request, and the user's identity, Next Labs ABAC/DAM allows organizations to control access to data, business transactions, and batch processes based on defined policies.

With SAP DAM, any changes in the attributes of the data or the user are dynamically considered, and the relevant policies are applied in real-time to enforce fine-grained access controls across various business functions. For example, a rule may specify that only US-based employees can access ITAR-classified materials from US locations. When a user attempts to access such materials, this rule is validated in real-time, ensuring that access is granted only to authorized individuals who meet the specified criteria.

Through the integration of Next Labs ABAC/DAM with SAP systems, organizations can effectively protect their sensitive data, maintain compliance, and enforce granular access controls across a wide range of business operations.

SAP Data sphere

This is optional but we can use this if you want to provide flexible predictive analytics to the users.

SAP BTP AI Launch Pad

This is optional and can be used to identify repeat breach patterns, time and detect security data anomalies in advance and add further access controls. 

MVP- Bill of Material Data Mesh

In the world of aerospace and defense, organizations face the challenge of managing bill of material (BOM) data across multiple systems. R By integrating diverse systems such as SAP S/4HANA, Team Center, and Siemens, they created a unified network of interconnected data mesh. This will enable seamless collaboration among internal and external engineering, supply chain, and product sales teams. With real-time visibility into BOMs, teams made informed decisions, optimized designs, synchronized manufacturing, and tailored offerings. The BOM data mesh can empower the organizations to achieve faster product development cycles, reduced costs, and improved customer satisfaction. 

Step 1: Define Role Requirements, Meta-data catalo Data Governance and access policies for BOM in Collibra

  • Identify the specific role requirements based on your organization's needs and compliance regulations.
  • Determine the attributes that will be used for access control, such as user roles, data sensitivity, and contextual factors.

Step 2: Understand BOM Creation and Editing Requirements

  • The organization requires that only users with specific engineering roles can create and edit BOMs. Additionally, certain fields in the BOM may be restricted for external supply chain users based on data sensitivity, such as pricing information.
  • Determine the functional access (actions user can perform) and data access (data records or fields users can see) and governance (rules for access)

Step 3: Define SAP S/4HANA Role

  • Create a custom role named "BOM Specialist" or copy standard role in SAP S/4HANA.
    Assign the authorization object M_BOM_GRP to the role, which controls access to BOM groups.
  • Assign transaction codes CS01 (Create BOM) and CS02 (Change BOM) to allow users with this role to perform BOM creation and editing tasks.

Step 4: Configure Next Lab ABAC DAM

SAP Next Lab ABAC DAM works natively with SAP and manages authorization logic through an externalized, standards-based policy framework. For instance, a rule may state, “Allow only US-based employees to access ITAR-classified materials from US locations.” When a user attempts to access materials, this rule is validated in real-time before access is granted.

  •  Configure Next Lab ABAC with attributes such as "User Role," "Data Sensitivity Level," and "Contextual Factors."
  •  Define "User Role" as an attribute to determine the user's role in the organization.
  •  Define "Data Sensitivity Level" as an attribute to classify BOMs based on their sensitivity, such as "Confidential" or "Public."
  •  Define "Contextual Factors" as attributes to consider additional factors, such as project or department.

Step 5: Integrate SAP GRC and Next Lab ABAC

This MVP use case leverages SAP GRC Access Control and SAP authorization for Governance and Functional Authorization and leverages ABAC for Data Authorization. It combines the features and fully integrated capabilities of SAP GRC Access Control and SAP authorization, such as ease of user assignment and role management, to efficiently supporting data attributes and avoiding the “role explosion” and custom development that would otherwise be necessary and costly.

  • Integrate SAP GRC and Next Lab ABAC to synchronize roles and access control policies.
  •  Map the "BOM Specialist" role in SAP GRC to the corresponding role in Next Lab ABAC, ensuring consistency in access control.

Step 5: Define ABAC Policies

  •  Define ABAC policies in Next Lab ABAC to enforce attribute-based access control for BOM creation and editing.
  •  Create a policy that allows users with the "BOM Specialist" role (User Role attribute) to create and edit BOMs.
  • Create a policy that restricts access to certain fields in the BOM based on the "Data Sensitivity Level" attribute and user profile policy (Ex: Manufacturing, Engineering, Supply Chain).

Step 6: Test and Validate the Role using AI

  • Train the model on different user profiles to validate data access using SAP BTP AI Launch Pad
  • Train the model further to auto-correct access issues and detect patterns to suggest changes to role access profiles (don’t let AI implement dynamic role access changes as it can be dangerous 😊)
  • Perform supervised automated test to test the “BOM Specialist" role by assigning it to a user and verifying that they can successfully create and edit BOMs.
  •  Validate that the ABAC policies.

Step 7: Integration SAP BTP Identity Access Governance to Active Directory

By integrating SAP BTP Identity Access Governance directly, users can seamlessly access data from multiple systems, including SAP S/4HANA, Team Center, Siemens, and other engineering, manufacturing, and supply chain systems. This integration enables a cohesive data mesh approach, allowing users to view and manage bill of materials across various systems.

Step 8: Integrate Collibra and Datasphere to Monetize and Publish bill of material insights to engineering, supply chain and product sales team.

You have the ability to define and design self-service analytic insight reports, which can be monetized and shared with both your internal and external engineering, supply chain, and product sales teams.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q1 ‘24 Release Highlights
entry.content:
SAP Commerce Cloud kicked off the new year with many exciting features to help our customers drive profitable growth! Time and time again, we’ve heard how critical it is for organizations to provide connected, seamless experiences across all digital and physical channels, while driving profitable engagements and increasing lifetime value.

These themes were reiterated at the recently concluded Shoptalk event in March, where SAP showcased how organizations can drive agility and end to end processes by connecting their commerce, marketing, sales, and service data with back-office ERP data. Customers also saw how they could harness the power of insightful and AI-powered scenarios to future-proof their businesses and drive profitable growth with the power of adaptive composability. I am also extremely excited that we announced SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework, a new solution that extends payment options for customers. You will find more details for this later in this post.

Let’s dive in a bit more and have a look at the top features that have been released in the last 3 months for SAP Commerce Cloud. For more information and to see the product in action, take a look at the Q1 2024 Highlights webcast to learn more about our latest innovations.

 

Industry-Tailored

Achieve Profitable and Accurate Shipments Through Flexible Order Orchestration

Order Management Services empower organizations to seamlessly integrate all owned or third-party purchasing channels with fulfillment channels for consistent order experiences, a unified view of inventory, and valuable insights across all touchpoints.  SAP Order Management Services helps ensure that your organization is meeting key goals for omni-channel order orchestration, is fulfilling customer promises reliably, and is utilizing data to understand relative performance and make improvements to the business.

Gain more control for orchestrating orders with deeper decision capabilities and easy to follow logic through no-code, low-code mechanism that speeds up implementation and drives agility. For more complex decisions, you can use any number of branches with a multitude of conditions with guaranteed scalability and performance. Order Management Services can provide real-time updates to business users and end customers based on order changes in backend systems. This provides more accurate visibility into the fulfillment of orders, allows a response to late customer change requests, and reduces returns by preventing unnecessary shipments.

Branching nodes for complex decisions

 

Improve Customer Satisfaction Through Flexible Sourcing Controls, Including Bundles

SAP Order Management Sourcing and Availability provides higher agility on sourcing products that might come as a group of products sold together in a bundle. We can now source products either as a full set or as individual products, removing complexity for shoppers. This enhances revenue generation by offering products both individually and as part of sets, simultaneously boosting customer satisfaction through the assurance of complete set availability. Newly added capabilities will allow organizations to configure the following:

  • Support for sourcing and availability of sets/bundles
  • Separate availability of each product of the set when required
  • Enforce the availability of full sets/bundles
  • Option for sourcing of all products in a set/bundle from the same location

Improve Operational Efficiency through Sourcing Analytics and Insights

We’ve added the ability to review past sourcing information to improve operations in a unified commerce environment. Order specialists can go back in time and analyze requested products, selected sites, and sourcing results more broadly. This helps to gain insights through analytics that can improve operational efficiency, all without having to re-run the sourcing logic.

Enhanced Financial Services Accelerator now available on SAP Store

SAP Commerce Cloud, financial services accelerator is designed to empower organizations in the financial services sector. With its self-service and commerce capabilities, customer experiences are simplified through digitalization, reducing duplication in data collection, and providing customers with personalized product options for easier purchase decision-making. Our modern composable storefront enables insurance and banking customers to quickly customize and build their own insurance and banking products using a set of predefined process templates. The inclusion of self-service capabilities allows customers to conveniently view their dashboard, compare quotes, change policies, schedule appointments with an agent, and perform other tasks results in time savings and a streamlined experience.

SAP Commerce Cloud, financial services accelerator

 

SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront, also received many recent enhancements that improve the B2B buying experience, the management of user profiles, and the search experience. These enhancements provide more control, save time, and make for a more intuitive experience.

B2B Quotes Provide Greater Control and Ease of B2B Transactions Between Buyers and Sellers

Have your sellers ever wanted to be able to start and view a B2B customer quote, and to minimize the back and forth needed to finalize the transaction? This is now available in a headless fashion for SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront, to streamline the B2B quote process.

B2B Commerce Quotes enable the negotiation of a final price for an order between buyers and sellers. Buyers can create and submit quote requests to sellers. Alternatively, sellers can also create quote requests for their buyers using the Assisted Service Module (ASM). This lets you open a new sales channel and increases your team’s productivity by negotiating digitally with full access to customer preferences. SAP CPQ is also supported in SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront, to empower your sales team and partners to offer complex product configurations, optimized pricing, and great-looking proposals.

Drive Productive and Intuitive Workflows with User Experience Enhancements

We have improved the user experience with My Account UI and Recent Searches to save users time on managing their profile and viewing search history. With My Account integration from SAP Customer Data Platform, shoppers can quickly and easily manage their personal details such as email address, password, notification preferences and consents, and view service request details. This, along with previous Customer Data Platform integrations such as those highlighted in the Q3 2023 release blog, further strengthens the benefits of connecting SAP solutions.

With Recent Searches functionality, shoppers no longer need to remember what they were searching for, and if it is still relevant. Shoppers can now revisit previously used search terms, saving time and frustration while providing a streamlined search experience. Recent Searches can boost conversion rates as shoppers are more likely to make a purchase when they see search phrases they have recently used and find relevant results quickly.

On a technical note, we are also using the newer <picture> tag instead of <img> throughout for better control and better-looking images at specific screen sizes. This enables responsive image selection for modern browsers that have support for srcset and media queries. But what if you are using legacy browsers? Not to worry because, as a fallback option when srcset is not provided, the media component will use the <img> tag.

Adopt Frontend and Backend Updates More Easily

Starting in February 2024, SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront uses the same continuous release numbering system as core commerce (2211.21 April 9, 2024), making it easy to align the frontend and backend update releases. There is now one place for all documentation, with one continuous documentation set, clear visibility of what’s new, and the same structure as core commerce, making it simple to find content.

Around February of each year, there will also be an annual framework update release to the newest Angular version. By having the framework update release around the same time each year, we are making the updates predictable. The framework was most recently updated to Angular v17 to keep up to date with compliance and provide faster performance. According to the Angular blog, v17 improvements include “deferrable views which brings performance and developer experience to the next level, up to 90% faster runtime with a built-in control flow loops in public benchmarks, and up to 87% faster builds for hybrid rendering and 67% for client-side rendering.”

 

Connected

Extend Payments Options with Open Payment Framework [Preview]

SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework, available in preview now, integrates with multiple third-party payment providers, including Stripe, Adyen, Worldpay and Airwallex. Organizations can easily adopt this plug-and-play solution, reducing integration complexity. The framework is extensible and headless, enabling independent operation of front and back ends, catering to diverse channel needs, and adding innovative solutions seamlessly. It covers common payment requirements and end-to-end payment processes across authorization, capture, refunds, and re-authorization and automatic updates with security and compliance standards. It also ensures customers have the freedom to pick their preferred payment method, which enhances the checkout experience.

As we take on the challenge of payments, we hope you are as excited as we, and our partners are about this, and enjoy this sneak preview before its General Availability release!

 

Insightful

Observe, Track, and Manage Performance of SAP Commerce Cloud Environments with Personal Access Token in Dynatrace

Customers can now access Dynatrace data via API to monitor the performance of their SAP Commerce Cloud environments. Personal Access Tokens enable customers to generate a token for API usage without administrator rights. The available scopes are bound to your permissions, which means you can only use the API counterparts of authorized features, and you can only access the data from authorized management zones.

View the documentation on how to generate the Personal Access Token.

Optimize Your SAP Commerce Cloud Infrastructure Utilization for Better Performance and Compliance

Have you ever wondered if your SAP Commerce Cloud infrastructure is performing at its best? SAP experts can help you be more effective with your infrastructure usage.

With Infrastructure Assessment Service for SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP experts will review your SAP Commerce Cloud environment and highlight any areas where the infrastructure is being most heavily utilized. Specialists from SAP will dig into these areas to identify potential improvements that could reduce and optimize the use of infrastructure while enabling efficient operations for peak seasonal loads.

 

Adaptive

Painless Kubernetes Upgrades Minimizes Disruption

SAP Commerce Cloud will now remain accessible and operational throughout upgrade process with no downtime. Customers can get the latest security and performance patches without interruption from our new cloud upgrades, meaning full continuity for your customers’ shopping experiences.

What’s more is that these changes are transparent and do not require testing of your implementation. All new environment upgrades will be executed with this improved method. You can read the FAQ in SAP for Me for more details.

New SAP Store Additions Bring Innovation and Cutting-edge Solutions

Recent additions to the SAP Store bring more innovation to our ever-expanding ecosystem, enhancing your digital experiences. We are excited to spotlight OneView Commerce that enables seamless omnichannel order execution. Omnichannel order execution requires coordinating across many channels and optimizing organizational resources, with the goal of delighting the customer with a seamless, convenient shopping experience that can create loyalty and high customer lifetime value.

  • Oneview Unified Commerce Platform extends SAP Order Management Services into the physical store to maximize profitability and improve customer outcomes. Oneview Unified Commerce Platform provides shopping convenience by supporting all in-store fulfillment types for web-based orders ensuring that more orders are fulfilled rapidly and eliminates cancellations. Order types include pick up in store, reserve in store, ship from store with major carrier integration. Associate-optimized workflows also allow for efficient pick and pack orders and preparing them for customer pick up or to ship directly to the customer using built-in carrier integrations.  Finally, if you have your own last mile delivery fleet, Oneview Commerce provides an intuitive delivery driver solution for at home deliveries.

Oneview Unified Commerce Platform

 

  • Are you a new customer that needs your commerce site to go-live quickly? SAP CX Services has an Accelerated Implementation Service for SAP Commerce Cloud that enables just that. The site can be up and running in only nine weeks! SAP CX Services can help you to fast track to value by offering a fixed scope, proven and reliable approach to implement standardized features with the flexibility built in to provide you with a solution that feels unique to your business.

Finally, we have some great news to share. We are extending our end of support for deprecated Accelerator UIs to Q3 2028 (previously stated as no earlier than 2025), and you can see the updated SAP Help documentation for more details. We will also have a dedicated webcast where we will cover the technical details of this topic in depth. Keep an eye out for calendar dates in our newsletter.

For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources:

  • Q1 2024 SAP Commerce Cloud Release Readiness Kit
  • Performance Optimization Best Practices
  • SAP Commerce Cloud update cycle
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Navigator single access point to release content

SAP Help Portal

  • SAP Commerce Cloud What’s New

Composable Commerce

  • SAP Report: Achieving Agility in Digital Commerce: Guidance to Help Organizational Leaders Future-Proof Digital Experiences with an Agile Commerce Approach

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow our SAP Commerce Cloud topic page to connect with the community!

entry.title: Output Type SPED trigger Inbound Delivery after PGI for Inter-Company STO's Outbound delivery
entry.content:
Inter-company purchasing is a very common business scenario in many SAP projects. In project practice, inter-company STO (or cross-company STO) is used to realize this process.                                     Different projects have different practices:

  • In some projects, one-step transfer is adopted, that is, after the issue company post goods issues by VL02N, the receiving company automatically completes the goods receipt posting.
  • In some projects, two-step transfer is adopted, that is, the issue company first performs goods issue posting, and then the receiving company performs goods receipt posting separately.
  • In some projects, inbound delivery needs to be created on the receiving side. Through the system settings, SAP can automatically trigger the inbound delivery document in this scenario after the goods issue posted by the issue company.

In this document we will discuss how to use the SAP standard output type SPED, to realize the function of automatically triggering the inbound delivery after the goods issue of outbound delivery is posted.              The Configuration Steps we have to maintain:                                                                             Maintain Output Types:                                                                                                           Configuration: SPRO –> IMG –> Logistics Execution –> Shipping –> Basic Shipping Functions –> Output Control –> Output Determination –> Maintain Output Determination for Outbound Deliveries –> Maintain Output Types"SPED" is the standard Output Type.Double click on the Output Type “SPED”.                                                                                                      These are the standard settings for the Output Type SPED.Maintain Output determination procedure: Configuration: SPRO –> IMG –> Logistics Execution –> Shipping –> Basic Shipping Functions  –> Output Control –> Output Determination –> Maintain Output Determination for Outbound Deliveries –> Maintain Output Determination ProcedureV10000 is the Standard Procedure. We can Create the New procedure by clicking on the New Entries or by Copying from the standard one. Select the Procedure and click on the control data field as shown.We can assign the condition type as “SPED” by clicking on the New Entries as shown.                            Enter Step as –> 50, Count as –> 1. After Maintaining the data click on the save button.Remember that for the requirement 408/1, the inbound delivery will be triggered AFTER goods issue posting for outbound delivery.                                                                                                                    Assign Output Determination Procedures:                                                                                              Configuration: SPRO –> IMG –> Logistics Execution –> Shipping –> Basic Shipping Functions –> Output Control –> Output Determination –> Maintain Output Determination for Outbound Deliveries –> Assign Output Determination ProceduresAssign Output Type as “SPED” to the Output Determination Procedure and delivery Type. After Completion of assigning click on the save button.

  • Note: Before Maintaining the Condition record, Maintain the back-end settings required for Inter-Company or Cross-Company STO. (Maintain the settings Assign the Delivery Type (NLCC) to the Document type and Supplying Plant.)
  • If we can’t maintain the setting, we can’t assign the partner in the Condition Record. The system throws an Error that Customer Master Data does not exist.

Maintain the Condition Record:                                                                                                            Enter output Type as “SPED”.

Make sure that the value of the field Partner cannot be empty in this condition record. After Maintaining the required data click on the save button.Assign Receiving Point for Inbound Deliveries:                                                                    Configuration:  SPRO –> IMG –> Logistics Execution –> Shipping –> Basic Shipping Functions –> Shipping Point and Goods Receiving Point Determination –> Assign Goods Receiving Points for Inbound DeliveriesWe can maintain the Shipping Points to the Plant and storage location by clicking on the New Entries or by copying from the standard one.

  • We need to assign shipping point for all receiving plant/storage location combination.

After Assign the data click on the save button. Set Up Confirmation Control:                                                                                                  Configuration: SPRO –> IMG –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Confirmations –> Set UP Confirmation controlWe can create the Confirmation Control Key by clicking on the New Entries or by Copying from the Standard one. Select the Confirmation Control Key and click on the Confirmation Sequence.

  • If We copy from the Standard one the data will be updated automatically.
  • If We create by clicking on the New Entries, we need to maintain the data Manually.After Maintaining the data click on the save button.                                                                              Make sure that the check box GR-Relevant and GR Assignment Key are both selected.After Maintaining the back-end settings, Next Create the STO purchase order in ME21N.
  • Maintain the confirmation Control.

                                 Standard PO created under the number 4500000902.

  • Create the Outbound Delivery in VL10D/VL10B
  • With Reference to the Outbound Delivery Number Post the Goods Issue in VL02N

We can check whether our Output Type is triggered for Outbound Delivery in VL03N.Now check whether the inbound Delivery number is triggered or not in the Purchase Order. (Go to ME23N). The inbound delivery number is automatically triggered in the Purchase order.

Remaining process is same, The Receiving Plant will do the MIGO. Check the inbound Delivery in VLO3N.

  • We can do this Scenario for both Inter and Intra Company Code STO’s
  • We can do this Scenario for only Two-Step process.
  • If we can try it for one step process, we will get an error like this No goods receipt is possible for Purchase Order.

entry.title: The Heterogeneous, Composable, Autonomous Enterprise
entry.content:
In "The Heterogeneous, Composable, Autonomous Enterprise" talk at the Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum 2024, Damien Johnson explores the transformational journey of enterprise technology from monolithic systems to the dawn of a new era characterized by heterogeneity, composability, and autonomy. Johnson provides insights into the evolving business landscape, where leveraging a mix of technologies and fostering flexible, adaptable operations are crucial for success. He emphasizes the significance of embracing change, integrating diverse systems, and moving towards more autonomous business processes. Through this lens, Johnson offers a forward-looking perspective on how enterprises can navigate the complexities of today's digital world, advocating for sustainability, resilience, and the strategic use of SAP and other technologies to craft dynamic, future-ready organizations.

The talk “The Heterogeneous, Composable, Autonomous Enterprise” by Damien Johnson weaves through several significant themes that are pivotal to understanding the future direction of enterprise technology and business operations.

Here are the main themes distilled from the speech:

The Evolution of Enterprise Technology: The speech traces the journey of enterprise technology from mainframes to the cloud era, emphasizing the rapid pace of change and the shift towards more efficient, interconnected, and autonomous systems.

Heterogeneous IT Landscapes: Acknowledging the complexity of modern IT landscapes, the talk underlines that successful enterprises now operate in environments that are not solely dependent on SAP or any single vendor but are a mix of various technologies and solutions. This heterogeneity is not just a challenge but a reality that businesses must embrace to stay competitive.

Composability in Enterprise Operations: The concept of composable enterprise is highlighted as crucial for businesses to adapt and thrive in a fast-changing world. This approach allows organizations to reconfigure and adapt their operations and processes with agility, leveraging modular, interchangeable components.

Autonomy and Automation: Johnson discusses the shift towards autonomous operations, where businesses increasingly rely on algorithms, AI, and machine learning to make decisions and execute processes. This move towards automation is not just about efficiency but about enabling higher-level decision-making and operations that can operate with minimal human intervention.

Cloud and Cloud ERP as Enablers: The role of cloud technology, particularly Cloud ERP, is emphasized as a key enabler for achieving heterogeneity, composability, and autonomy in enterprises. SAP's efforts to adapt and lead in this space, with initiatives like Cloud ERP and integration capabilities, are positioned as vital for supporting businesses in this new era.

Sustainability and Resilience: Sustainability and the ability to build resilient operations are identified as critical outcomes that modern enterprises should aim for. The talk touches on how technological evolution supports these goals, enabling businesses to be more agile, efficient, and responsible in their operations.

Adapting to Change: A recurring theme throughout the talk is the necessity for businesses and technology architectures to be adaptable to change. Johnson emphasizes the importance of being able to quickly respond to new challenges and opportunities, leveraging the heterogeneous, composable, and autonomous capabilities of modern enterprise systems.

These themes collectively point towards a future where flexibility, adaptability, and the intelligent use of technology define the successful enterprise. Johnson's talk offers insights into how SAP and its ecosystem can support organizations in navigating this complex, evolving landscape.

Remember:

"The era of monolithic systems is fading. Today, the spotlight is on heterogeneous, composable, and autonomous enterprises that thrive on adaptability, resilience, and innovation." - Damien Johnson

Access Presentation: Here         

Watch the YouTube Video of Damien's Presentation: Here  

CTA

entry.title: Career Corner Partner Chat with ADventas
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Today I’m very excited to share with you this partner chat I participated in a couple of months ago.

Together with Joshua Margo from SAP we discussed how to become a business consultant, especially for SAP GTM. I shared some general information about my company ADventas as well as some details on our onboarding process.  You can view the current ADventas job opportunity here.

You can find the partner chat episodes (2 parts) here:

Career Corner Partner Chat - Episode 1: ADventas (Part 1)

Career Corner Partner Chat - Episode 1: ADventas (Part 2)

In the interview we spoke about the following topics:

(Part 1)

  1. Tell us a little bit about yourself, your career path and how you came to work at ADventas?
  2. What are the current goals for ADventas?
  3. What does the right candidate look like and what are you willing to train for?
  4. How many customers are using Global Trade Management presently?
  5. Where is your company based and where are your customers located?

(Part 2)

  1. Where do you prefer your employees be located?
  2. How do you onboard new employees?
  3. What's different about working at ADventas as compared to anywhere else you have worked?
  4. How do you build trust among employees at ADventas?
  5. How many employees are currently at ADventas?
  6. If I do not fit all of the requirements, should I still apply for a position at ADventas?
  7. What are the common career paths at ADventas?
  8. Any parting words of advice for those interested in a career as a SAP consultant?

Please tune in if you’re interested. I’m looking forward to your thoughts and comments.

All the best,

Peter

entry.title: Intercompany Execution of Services (aka "Dual Order")
entry.content:
In a recent Blog post you learned about “Resource-Related Intercompany Billing” as a way to resolve cross company cost postings on Service Orders. That scenario addresses a use case where a technician from another Company Code posts time (and potentially expenses) on a Service Order. This may happen for example if the first company (left side) is not able to provide enough capacity, or does not have the necessary skill for certain tasks within the Service, but the overall coordination and ownership remains in Company 1.

Of course the cost re-allocation process is limited to a very specific use case and does not provide the “executing” Company with the means to properly plan and report on the Service provided. It simply sends the “warm bodies” to perform the work and offload their cost on Company 1’s Service Order.

To address a more general requirement we introduced the “Intercompany Execution” process in 2023. In this scenario, we enable the full delegation of a Service to another company code. i.e. if a customer facing entity (for example a Sales Subsidiary) is not able to fulfill a certain Service, it can be sent on to another company code and will result in a completely autonomous Service Order for the planning and execution of the requested Service.

This can be either be a “simple” Service Order:

Or, an “Advanced Execution” Order, using a Maintenance Order for the actual execution of the work.

This (second) order is then fully in the ownership of the execution entity to plan and execute on it. Items can be changed, added, again sent on to another company code etc.

The way we achieve this in the system is depicted in the diagram below.

As you can see, a Fix Price Item is created on the customer facing side. This item is a special Item Category that will create a corresponding Intercompany Service Order in an Execution Company specified via a “Executing Sales Org” field. This Intercompany Order can then be used for planning and execution of the Service, i.e. any changes can occur as long as the Intercompany reference is retained on all the related items. Once the Service is performed and subsequently billed, the resulting Invoice will be identified as “intercompany” and pass the account assignment of the original Fix Price Item to Accounts Payable on the “Commercial” side. This way all the cost flow back to the customer facing Order Item!

Lets go through step by step.

First, you need to define the relationship between sending and receiving Sales Organizations for each originating Item Category. The relationships defined here will then restrict the choice of recipients allowed when “sending” a Service Item.

In the Service Order itself you will find two new pre-defined Item Categories to start an intercompany process

  • SRVI with a fixed-price (service order-related billing) and
  • SVAI which allows you to use an ad-hoc billing plan.

When creating such an item, a new assignment block will available to maintain and view the intercompany details. Initially, before we can even release the item, a receiving sales Organization needs to be selected (from the ones defined in the IMG activity above).

Once the Intercompany Order is created, details will be available here as well.

On the header of the Intercompany Order the Customer is the Intercompany Customer for the ordering Company code.

The Item is copied with the original material and item category determination is performed in the Intercompany Order again. If there is configuration information existing on the item it will also be copied to the Intercompany Order and can be continued to be detailed out here. Please note that the material can be changed and additional items added to the order, as long as they carry the reference to the original item.

If more than one item from the original Service Order is to be sent to the same receiving sales org, they will be added to the same Intercompany Order, to prevent the creation of a flurry of individual orders on the executing side.

If a receiving Organization needs to once again distribute all or some of the Order to yet another Company, this is fully supported. Again only the reference to the original item is needed.

Revenue recognition is completely independent in the original and Intercompany Orders.

On the Customer-facing order (which is as we have learned currently a Fix Price order only!) only Completed Contract is currently supported as revenue recognition method.

On the Executing Order all billing types and supported revenue recognition methods are possible:

Finally, once the executing Company triggers the billing, the Company Code customer on the Intercompany Order is recognized by the system and the costs will be account assigned back to the original item on the Customer-facing Order. (i.e. the revenue of the executing company is equivalent to the cost on the original item)

entry.title: Part 4 - SAP MDG – A Stepping Stone for SAP S/4HANA Journey
entry.content:
Introduction

Clients across the globe are getting into the Transformation journey started with SAP S/4HANA.

In this blog, we will take one of the use cases to discuss how the master data (MDG)  journey will be the stepping stone for SAP S/4HANA Transformation and the needed steps to achieve it.

This is the 4th blog in this series. Please go through Part 1,2 & 3 in the given below link

https://blogs.sap.com/2023/01/27/sap-mdg-a-stepping-stone-for-sap-s-4hana-journey-part-i/

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-mdg-a-stepping-stone-for-sap-s-4hana-journey-part-2/ba-p/13579098

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-mdg-a-stepping-stone-for-sap-s-4hana-journey-part-3/ba-p/13651625

Key Aspects for MDG : Deep Dive

B. MDG Specific

B.2 User Interface

For any product/solution to be successful, it needs to have a good user experience. The first impression for the users comes from the user interface

SAP MDG offers Netweaver Business Client (NWBC) and SAP Fiori as the UI.

For this use case, NWBC is considered as it is tightly integrated with MDG data model which means the out of Box data model is already available in NWBC by standard

In this section, we will discuss the approach for UI flexibility for user experience and solution approach for field status for specific system

Approach for UI ( Global Form vs Specifics )

As MDG going to the central master data systems catering for 3 ECC systems & future S/4 system, it is important to finalize the strategy for UI as we are considering having all finalized fields ( Global + Local) be governed in MDG

There are 2 options which can be catered to this

  • Global Form – All finalized fields will be shown to all users irrespective of the ERP system ( Region) to which user belongs to
  • Regional specific Form – All global fields + that specific region fields are only shown.

 

 

It needs discussion with various stakeholders in terms of business value realization, use cases and vision before finalising the UI for MDG.

UI enhancement can be done either by configuration / developments.

 Solution Approach for Field Status Requirements

In the previous blog on data model section, there was a topic on field status when there are differences between ECC systems.

  • Field status matches across all systems – Same will be configured at back end ( Account Group level)
  • Field status having differences across systems - One of the suggestions is to keep Optional at the back-end configuration

This will help to overcome the forced need of data enrichment at local systems for data migration into MDG & also for the CRUD process.

If that’s the case, then comes the below question

How do we handle the need for field status for specific ECC systems?

The need for a specific ECC system field status ( Ex: Field 2 for ECC – Americas ; Field 3 for ECC – Asia to be mandatory for Acc grp 1) to be catered.

There are multiple solution approaches for this. Considering the complexity, there needs a framework solution which needs to give flexibility  and reusable

UI Framework

The high-level solution is to have a framework to manage the exceptions as below

 

 

  1. Acc grp Configuration Backend – By SAP standard, the field status are setup at MDG UI
  2. The framework solution gets MDG to read a UI control table and set the field status accordingly ( Exceptional)
  3. If there are no entries in control table , the field property will behave as per the status set at the account group configuration at the backend

The solution is to use the Field Properties BADI to read the control table to set the field properties.

Below are the example control table entries for the requirements listed above

If a field is at the organizational level, then the field status behaves once the company codes have been added.

What if , there are exceptions at general data ?

As the framework have company code as one of the dimensions ( input), in the form, that field will show as Optional at first. However, once the company code is added, the framework will trigger the exceptions status to show in the form.

It could be a training topic for the users ( They might wonder about the status change in the form during the process !)

Please be noted that for Block, Mark for Delete CR’s , by SAP standard all fields will be read-only except the actual block/mark for delete specific fields. So the framework solution will not be applicable for those 2 CR types.

Even though the solution offers flexibility , there are some limitations , disadvantages which it brings such as field status validations to be build, data load etc which will be discussed in the subsequent sections

In the next blog, I will cover other topics in detail.

About the Author,

Antony Prasanna has been working in SAP master data space since 2006. Have experience working across multiple large transformation programs on SAP S/4HANA including SAP MDM, MDG and Data Migration tools.

entry.title: Transforming SAP S/4HANA Implementations: A Leap Towards Efficiency with Automation and AI
entry.content:
In their insightful talk at the Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum 2024, Bob Cummings and Dean Milne, (both long-time SAP veterans) championed a transformative approach to SAP implementations, advocating for the early integration of automation and AI technologies. They highlighted the necessity of front-loading projects with early visibility of the customer’s working system utilizing comprehensive customer data to tailor solutions precisely to each enterprise's unique needs.

Cummings and Milne underscored the importance of leveraging SAP Best Practices and the SAP Clean Core as a foundation while embracing innovation and customization through automation. He emphasized collaboration with consulting and technology firms as crucial for scaling this approach, ensuring that more enterprises can benefit from efficient, automated implementation. This paradigm shift aims to reduce traditional implementation challenges, such as the late visibility of the final working system and avoid project delays and budget overruns, promising a future where SAP projects are more predictable, adaptable, and successful.

Quote

"As history shows, Automation is unlikely to replace humans but will significantly amplify what they are able to do. Enterprise IT Transformations that combine Human Intelligence with Intelligent Automation will be faster and more impactful to the Enterprise. It will allow for continuous transformation without the disruption.”  Bob Cummings

Key Themes

  • The Historical Challenge of SAP Implementations: The talk begins by acknowledging the longstanding challenges in SAP implementations, such as high failure rates, budget overruns, and project delays. These issues are framed as persistent frustrations within the industry that have not substantially changed over decades.

 

  • Automation and AI as a Solution: Bob Cummings & Dean Milne introduce automation and AI as pivotal technologies capable of addressing these historical challenges. The focus is on how these technologies can dramatically reduce risks, costs, and disruptions while simultaneously enhancing the value derived from SAP projects.

 

  • Transforming Project Implementation: A significant portion of the talk details a transformative approach to SAP project implementation, emphasizing early and continuous integration of automation and AI. This strategy aims to front-load projects with early visibility of the final working system through AI-Poweredautomation to avoid the common pitfalls of time and cost overruns, scope creep and late-stage project surprises.

 

  • Practical Application and Methodology: The methodology discussed involves taking a comprehensive inventory of customer data and processes, then applying automation and AI through an "automation hub" to rapidly configure and customize SAP systems according to specific enterprise needs. This approach is designed to yield a working, testable system early in the project lifecycle, enabling early adjustments and reducing the risk of late-stage failures.

 

  • Collaboration with Consulting Firms: The model also involves a collaborative effort with consulting firms to scale the approach, suggesting a shift in the traditional consulting model towards one that leverages automation and AI to enhance efficiency and outcomes.

 

  • Customer-Centricity and Customization: A recurring theme is the focus on deeply understanding and integrating customer-specific requirements and data from the outset, using automation to tailor the SAP system to the unique needs of each enterprise.

 

  • Best Practices and Standardization: The talk touches on leveraging SAP Best Practices as a foundation, while also highlighting the need for customization and adjustment based on individual company processes and realities. This balance between standardization and customization is presented as key to achieving successful outcomes.

 

  • Future Vision for SAP Implementations: Ultimately, the talk presents a future vision for SAP implementations where automation and AI play central roles in reducing traditional project risks while delivering greater value and efficiency. This vision includes an ecosystem where SAP systems are delivered faster, projects are more predictable, and enterprises can achieve their transformation goals more reliably.  Dramatically reduce risk, while at the same time dramatically elevating the value derived from SAP systems.

 

Slides and Access Full Presentation: Here         

CTA

entry.title: The Role of Enterprise Architecture to Manage SAP's Transformation into the Cloud
entry.content:
SAP’s Marco Michel provides an in-depth analysis of the critical role that Enterprise Architecture (EA) plays in SAP's transformation journey into the cloud. Drawing from SAP's internal experience, Michel outlines the strategies and methodologies that have underpinned this transformative process. He emphasizes the importance of aligning enterprise architecture with SAP's broader business strategy, advocating for a comprehensive understanding of this strategy among architects.

The presentation delves into the necessity of adopting a cloud strategy that supports standardization and automation, enabling SAP to shift from custom-tailored solutions towards a more scalable and efficient cloud service model. Michel highlights the implementation of a capability-driven approach to streamline processes, reduce complexity, and ensure that technology investments are directly tied to strategic business outcomes. This session underscores the significance of governance, data architecture, and a holistic view of the IT portfolio in navigating the challenges and seizing the opportunities presented by the cloud transition.

Main Themes

These themes collectively outline a comprehensive approach to managing SAP's transformation into the cloud, emphasizing strategic alignment, education, simplification, the role of enterprise architecture, governance, and the strategic use of data.

Strategic Alignment and Understanding: The transformation from a solution company to a true cloud company and the emphasis on standardized platform functionality available via subscription align with the strategic alignment theme. SAP's move towards a cloud-first strategy, while not exclusively cloud-only, is highlighted as part of their strategic direction.

Education and Mindset Shift: The talk and slides emphasize the importance of educating the team and broader organization on SAP’s strategic direction towards the cloud, which is crucial for ensuring alignment and fostering a culture of continuous learning and adaptation.

Simplification and Standardization: The slides underscore the move towards simplification and standardization as part of SAP's cloud strategy. This includes leveraging SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) as the foundation and following a principle of "one solution per capability" to support SAP's business capabilities.

Role of Enterprise Architecture: Enterprise Architecture's role is depicted as designing the end-to-end, enterprise-wide architecture to support business goals and ensuring that the target architecture and roadmap are aligned globally across processes, applications, and data.

Governance and Portfolio Management: The establishment of architecture governance mechanisms, such as the Architecture Review Board (ARB) and Design Review Board (DRB), to make educated architectural decisions, collect feedback, and ensure transparency is emphasized. This governance structure supports the strategic IT governance and portfolio management theme.

Data as a Strategic Asset: The importance of building a strong data foundation with the Intelligent Enterprise Data Platform and treating data as a valuable corporate resource is highlighted, aligning with the theme of treating data as a strategic asset.

Agility Within Governance: While the emphasis on agility within governance is not explicitly outlined in the slides, the introduction of governance boards and the strategic use of the LeanIX tool for architecture modeling suggest a framework that balances agility with the need for structured decision-making and alignment.

Remember:

"“The discipline of EA is more important than ever, a strong and business architecture focused EA Practice is key to drive SAP’s digital transformation” -  Marco Michel

Access Presentation: Here       

Watch the YouTube Video of Marco's Presentation: Here  

CTA
For Enterprise Architect SAP Community Updates and More: Connect with and Follow Paul Kurchina - https://www.linkedin.com/in/paulkurchina/ 

 

Flag Photo with Marco Michel

entry.title: Filling the Holes in your S/4 Business Case
entry.content:
In "Filling the Holes in Your S/4 Business Case," presented at the Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum 2024, Stuart Browne of Resulting IT offers a fresh perspective on building compelling business cases for SAP S/4HANA implementations. Through an engaging blend of storytelling, historical innovation lessons, and user-centered design principles, Browne emphasizes the critical importance of aligning technology projects with strategic business objectives, the power of narrative to bridge complex technology and business value, and the necessity of simplifying communication to unlock stakeholder support.

Highlighting innovative visual tools and the significance of emotional engagement, Browne's talk advocates for a customer-centric approach that prioritizes understanding and addressing the real-world needs of end-users. By focusing on the business value, involving end-users early, leveraging the power of storytelling, and aligning closely with corporate strategy, organizations can navigate the challenges of SAP business case creation and ensure their technology investments drive meaningful innovation and competitive advantage.

Stuart presented with no slides - just some props.

Key Themes

The talk advocates for a customer-centric approach to building business cases, underlined by strategic alignment, the value of storytelling, and the need for simplicity in conveying complex technical solutions.

  • The Role of Emotional Engagement: Stuart highlighted the importance of connecting with the audience on an emotional level. This aspect can be emphasized more in your analysis by noting that understanding and leveraging the emotional drivers behind stakeholders' decisions are critical for securing support for SAP S/4HANA projects.
  • The Innovative Use of Visual Tools: The use of hexagons as a metaphor and practical tool for visualization in building business cases was a unique point in Stuart's presentation. It might be worth mentioning how innovative visualizations can aid in simplifying complex ideas and engaging stakeholders.
  • The Historical Perspective on Innovation: The detailed exploration of the history of drills served as a metaphor for technological evolution and innovation. This analogy underpins the importance of seeing beyond the tool itself to its application and impact. Your analysis could highlight how historical perspectives on tool evolution can provide insights into current technological implementations.
  • The Need for Strategic Business Cases: The talk underscored the necessity of creating business cases that are not just tied to the technical aspects of SAP S/4HANA but are deeply integrated with the strategic objectives and competitive advantages of the organization. It might be beneficial to explicitly state that strategic alignment should drive the formulation of business cases, rather than starting with the solution (S/4HANA) and working backward.
  • The Importance of Predicting Future Trends: Stuart discussed using tools like Wardley Mapping to predict how technology evolves from a custom product to a commodity. This approach to understanding and anticipating the future trajectory of technological solutions could be highlighted as a recommendation for building forward-looking business cases that align with long-term strategic goals.

Main Recommendations

These recommendations are designed to help attendees build compelling, strategic, and user-focused business cases for SAP S/4HANA implementations.

  • Focus on Business Value: Prioritize projects that clearly contribute to strategic business objectives.
  • Engage End Users: Involve end users early in the project to ensure solutions meet their needs and to foster adoption.
  • Use Storytelling: Leverage the power of narratives to communicate the benefits and impact of the project to stakeholders.
  • Simplify the Message: Make complex technical details accessible and relatable to a non-technical audience.
  • Demonstrate Quick Wins: Identify and highlight early successes to build momentum and support for the project.
  • Align with Corporate Strategy: Ensure the business case is directly linked to the organization's strategic goals and competitive advantage.
  • Emphasize Innovation: Highlight how the project will drive innovation and long-term value for the organization.

Remember:

"Learn how to think differently about your S/4 journey to build better executive commitment.” -  Stuart Browne

Watch Stuart’s Entertaining YouTube video: Here 

CTA

For Enterprise Architect SAP Community Updates and More: Connect with and Follow Paul Kurchina - https://www.linkedin.com/in/paulkurchina/ 

Photos from Stuart's Talk

entry.title: Where can I find learning materials to manage various aspects of financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
We  recommend to access our Designing the Record-to-Report Process in SAP S/4HANA learning journey learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey provides business users and application consultants the necessary skills to configure Accruals Management and Financial Statement Reporting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, also including the option of hands-on learning to deepen and practice your learning experience in our training system.

Learning objectives
Acquire the skills and knowledge to manage various aspects of financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA, including creation and use of semantic tags, execution of balance sheet and income statement reports, analysis of cash flow, and tracking changes in General Ledger Accounts as per business requirements.

Prerequisites

  • Outlining the Record-to-Report Process in SAP S/4HANA

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.
